name: Bali
description: A magical island paradise blending lush rice terraces with vibrant Hindu culture and world-class surf breaks.

name: Patagonia
description: One of the world's last great wildernesses — glaciers, jagged peaks, and endless wind-swept steppes.

name: Santorini
description: Iconic blue-domed churches and cliffside white buildings cascading above the volcanic caldera.

name: Kyoto
description: Japan's cultural heart — home to over 1000 Buddhist temples, lush bamboo forests, and the timeless tea ceremony.

name: Machu Picchu
description: The legendary Incan citadel floating above the clouds in the Andes — a UNESCO World Heritage wonder.

name: Reykjavik
description: Gateway to Iceland's surreal landscapes — geysers, black sand beaches, and the Aurora Borealis.

name: Safari Kenya
description: Witness the Great Wildebeest Migration across the Masai Mara — nature's greatest spectacle.

name: Amalfi Coast
description: A UNESCO coastline of dazzling villages perched on dramatic cliffs above the turquoise Tyrrhenian Sea.

name: Queenstown
description: The adventure capital of the world — bungee jumping, skydiving, and skiing against stunning alpine backdrops.

name: Marrakech
description: A sensory feast of winding medina alleys, vibrant souks, aromatic spices, and ornate palaces.

name: Maldives
description: Crystal-clear turquoise lagoons and pristine coral reefs in the ultimate barefoot-luxury paradise.

name: Prague
description: A fairy-tale city of Gothic spires, Baroque palaces, and cobblestone streets spanning the Vltava River.

name: Banff
description: Turquoise glacial lakes and soaring Rocky Mountain peaks in Canada's oldest national park.

name: Dubrovnik
description: The Pearl of the Adriatic — a perfectly preserved medieval walled city on the Dalmatian Coast.

name: Tokyo
description: A relentless electrifying megacity where ancient shrines and neon-lit streets coexist in harmony.

name: Petra
description: The legendary Rose City — a 2000-year-old Nabataean marvel carved directly into rose-red sandstone cliffs.

name: Cape Town
description: Where two oceans meet — Table Mountain, the Cape Winelands, and the vibrant V&A Waterfront.

name: Phuket
description: Thailand's largest island — stunning Andaman beaches, emerald sea, and legendary Thai street food.

name: Amsterdam
description: A city of masterpiece museums, charming canal houses, and a liberal vibrant arts scene.

name: Havana
description: A living time capsule of vintage Cadillacs, crumbling colonial grandeur, and irresistible salsa rhythms.

name: Swiss Alps
description: Pristine ski slopes and dramatic alpine scenery in the heart of Swiss skiing culture.

name: Angkor Wat
description: The world's largest religious monument — a breathtaking 12th-century Khmer empire temple complex.

name: Barcelona
description: Gaudi's organic masterpieces, La Rambla's buzz, and the sun-drenched Barceloneta beach in one city.

name: Rio de Janeiro
description: The Marvelous City — Copacabana, Christ the Redeemer, and the world's greatest Carnival.

name: Zanzibar
description: A spice island paradise blending white-sand beaches, turquoise Indian Ocean waters, and Swahili culture.

name: Lisbon
description: Seven hilly neighborhoods of colorful azulejo tiles, vintage trams, and the melancholic sound of Fado.

name: Galapagos Islands
description: A living museum of extraordinary wildlife — giant tortoises, marine iguanas, and Darwin's finches.

name: Istanbul
description: Where East meets West — Hagia Sophia, the Grand Bazaar, and the shimmering Bosphorus Strait.

name: Hallstatt
description: A jaw-dropping Alpine lakeside village — often called the most beautiful village in the world.

name: New Orleans
description: The soulful birthplace of Jazz — Mardi Gras, beignets, and Bourbon Street's electric nightlife.

name: Mount Cook
description: New Zealand's highest peak surrounded by glaciers — a certified Dark Sky Reserve for breathtaking stargazing.

name: Chiang Mai
description: Northern Thailand's cultural crown jewel — hundreds of ornate temples and elephant sanctuaries.

name: Torres del Paine
description: The crown jewel of Chilean Patagonia — iconic granite towers, glaciers, and pristine lakes.

name: Cinque Terre
description: Five candy-colored fishing villages clinging to dramatic Ligurian cliffs above the Italian Riviera.

name: Norwegian Fjords
description: UNESCO-listed fjords of staggering beauty — steep cliffs, mirror-like waters, and remote mountain villages.

name: Varanasi
description: The world's oldest living city — sacred ghats along the Ganges and the eternal cycle of life and death.

name: Phi Phi Islands
description: Emerald waters, towering limestone karsts, and the world-famous Maya Bay.

name: Bhutan
description: The Last Shangri-La — a Buddhist kingdom measuring happiness in the pristine Himalayas.

name: Tuscany
description: Rolling hills dotted with vineyards, cypress trees, and medieval hilltop towns.

name: Oludeniz
description: Home to Turkey's most photographed beach — the Blue Lagoon and the world's premier paragliding site.

name: Scottish Highlands
description: Ancient lochs, rugged moorland, and medieval castles in Britain's last great wilderness.

name: Siem Reap
description: Gateway to the ancient temples of Angkor — the greatest archaeological marvel in Southeast Asia.

name: Palawan
description: An untouched paradise of turquoise lagoons, white beaches, and the UNESCO Puerto Princesa river.

name: Iceland Ring Road
description: Drive the epic 1400km Route 1 circumnavigating the island — waterfalls, glaciers, and volcanic landscapes.

name: Sahara Morocco
description: Journey to the edge of the Sahara — camel treks to towering sand dunes and nights in desert camps.

name: Oaxaca
description: Mexico's cultural and culinary capital — mezcal, vibrant markets, and Dia de Muertos.

name: Ha Long Bay
description: A UNESCO World Heritage seascape of 1600 emerald limestone islands rising from the Gulf of Tonkin.

name: Serengeti
description: Africa's most iconic safari destination — endless plains teeming with the Big Five and the Great Migration.

name: Bruges
description: A perfectly preserved medieval gem of cobblestone streets and world-class Belgian chocolate.

name: Hoi An
description: A magical ancient trading port of colorful lanterns and some of Vietnam's finest cuisine.

name: Meteora
description: Monasteries perched impossibly atop towering rock pillars — one of the most astonishing sights on Earth.

name: Costa Rica
description: A biodiversity hotspot of cloud forests, active volcanoes, and pristine Pacific and Caribbean beaches.

name: Antelope Canyon
description: The photographer's holy grail — swirling sandstone slot canyons in Arizona that glow with ethereal light.

name: Blue Lagoon Iceland
description: A milky-blue geothermal spa in a prehistoric lava field.

name: Kruger Park
description: South Africa's flagship safari — the Big Five in their most accessible and iconic setting.

name: Kathmandu
description: The gateway to the Himalayas — chaotic spiritual and bursting with ancient temples.

name: Positano
description: The jewel of the Amalfi Coast — pastel-hued houses tumbling down a cliff into the sea.

name: Tulum
description: A boho paradise of Mayan ruins on clifftops, crystal cenotes, and Caribbean beaches.

name: Taipei
description: A vibrant city of night markets and some of Asia's most spectacular street food.

name: Cappadocia
description: A dreamlike landscape of fairy chimneys and cave dwellings — best from a hot air balloon.

name: Lake Bled
description: A fairy-tale emerald lake with a tiny island church and a clifftop castle in the Julian Alps.

name: Lofoten Islands
description: Dramatic Arctic archipelago of jagged peaks and some of the Northern Lights' best displays.

name: Ubud
description: Bali's spiritual and artistic heart — rice terraces, yoga retreats, and the Sacred Monkey Forest.

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The world's largest coral reef system — an underwater paradise of extraordinary marine biodiversity.

name: Jiuzhaigou
description: A magical valley of multi-colored lakes, waterfalls, and snow-capped peaks in Sichuan Province.

name: Salar de Uyuni
description: The world's largest salt flat — a mirror-like expanse that perfectly reflects the sky.

name: El Nido
description: Hidden lagoons, secret beaches, and dramatic karst cliffs in Palawan's most pristine island paradise.

name: Chefchaouen
description: The Blue City — a labyrinth of strikingly blue-washed streets tucked into the Rif Mountains.

name: Wadi Rum
description: The Valley of the Moon — a vast desert of soaring sandstone mountains and ancient petroglyphs.

name: Plitvice Lakes
description: A UNESCO cascade of 16 terraced turquoise lakes connected by thundering waterfalls in dense forest.

name: Inca Trail
description: The world's most iconic trek — four days through cloud forest and Andean passes to Machu Picchu.

name: Socotra
description: Earth's most alien-looking island — Dragon Blood Trees and endemic species found nowhere else.

name: Faroe Islands
description: Remote Atlantic archipelago of emerald cliffs and roaring waterfalls in swirling mist.

name: Bagan
description: A vast plain of over 2000 ancient Buddhist temples — best seen from a hot air balloon.

name: Sossusvlei
description: The world's tallest sand dunes — towering red-orange giants rising from the ancient Namib Desert.

name: Kotor
description: One of Europe's best-preserved medieval towns — a UNESCO bay of stunning natural beauty.

name: Yellowstone
description: America's first national park — Old Faithful, prismatic hot springs, grizzly bears, and wolf packs.

name: Darjeeling
description: The Queen of the Hills — emerald tea gardens, the Toy Train, and panoramic Himalayan views.

name: Guilin
description: Legendary alien karst peaks rising from the Li River — arguably China's most iconic landscape.

name: Dolomites
description: A UNESCO mountain range of impossibly dramatic pinnacles glowing pink at sunset.

name: Pamukkale
description: Snow-white travertine terraces of warm mineral pools — Turkey's surreal Cotton Castle.

name: Easter Island
description: A remote volcanic island of over 1000 colossal stone Moai statues carved by the ancient Rapa Nui.

name: Similan Islands
description: Thailand's finest diving destination — pristine coral reefs in the Andaman Sea.

name: Munnar
description: Kerala's glorious hill station — an emerald sea of rolling tea plantations in the Western Ghats.

name: Valletta
description: Europe's smallest capital — a UNESCO Baroque city of grandmasters palaces and fortress walls.

name: Zhangjiajie
description: The real-life Avatar mountains — towering sandstone pillar columns wrapped in mist in Hunan Province.

name: Big Island Hawaii
description: Witness live lava flows at Hawaii Volcanoes National Park and snorkel alongside manta rays.

name: Jaisalmer
description: The Golden City — a living desert fortress of sandstone rising from the Thar Desert.

name: Yosemite
description: America's most beloved valley — El Capitan, Half Dome, and thundering waterfalls.

name: Penang
description: Asia's food capital — a UNESCO heritage city of street food, colonial shophouses, and street art.

name: Skeleton Coast
description: One of the world's most desolate and hauntingly beautiful coastlines — shipwrecks and desert lions.

name: Rotorua
description: New Zealand's geothermal wonderland — boiling mud pools, geysers, and immersive Maori culture.

name: Siwa Oasis
description: A remote oasis of date palms in Egypt's Western Desert — oracle temple of Alexander the Great.

name: Hvar
description: Croatia's sunniest island of lavender fields, Renaissance piazzas, and glamorous beach clubs.

name: Alberobello
description: A surreal UNESCO village of whitewashed trulli cone-roofed houses.

name: Kuala Lumpur
description: A dynamic multicultural metropolis with Petronas Towers, vibrant street food markets, and rich Malay culture.

name: Cinque Terre Alt
description: Dramatic coastal villages with colorful buildings and excellent Ligurian seafood cuisine.

name: Koh Samui
description: Thailand's tropical resort island with palm-fringed beaches, luxury spas, and vibrant beach clubs.

name: Hampi
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site of 14th-century Vijayanagara Empire ruins scattered across a boulder-strewn landscape.

name: Fjordland NP
description: New Zealand's most spectacular national park — Milford Sound, Doubtful Sound, and Sutherland Falls.
